Bandidos Motorcycle Club.description: The Bandidos Motorcycle Club is an outlaw motorcycle gang designated by the U.S. Department of Justice, involved in drug trafficking, weapons trafficking, murder, and other serious crimes. Active primarily in the Northwestern, Southeastern, Southwestern, and West Central regions of the United States, with an estimated 800 to 1,000 members across 16 states. The club has been supplied with drugs by Los Zetas and has associated with the Outlaws, while rivaling groups such as the Hells Angels and Mongols.

Flying Dragons.description: Chinese American street gang prominent in New York City's Chinatown from the 1970s to early 1990s. Formed in 1967 by immigrants from Hong Kong, affiliated with the Hip Sing Tong. Engaged in drug trafficking, extortion, murder, and illegal gambling. Heavily involved in heroin trafficking after the Pizza Connection prosecutions. Indicted on federal racketeering charges in 1994, effectively dismantled.

Kurdistan Workers' Party.description: Kurdish militant political organization and armed guerrilla group primarily based in southeastern Turkey, northern Iraq, and northeastern Syria. Founded on 27 November 1978 by Abdullah Öcalan, initially seeking an independent Kurdish state but later shifting to autonomy and cultural rights within Turkey. Designated as a terrorist organization by Turkey, the US, EU, and others. In March 2025, declared a ceasefire and announced plans for dissolution.

descriptions: Retaliation for the attack on Lanza, indicating conflict between the two batteries.

descriptions: Sinesi-Francavilla attacks Moretti-Pellegrino-Lanza affiliate Albanese, leading to further violence.

Nel bar H24 di via san Severo, entrano due sicari che sparano all'indirizzo di tre persone tra cui proprio Giuseppe Albanese, che però scappa e riesce a chiudersi in bagno. Viene ucciso Roberto Tizzano e ferito Roberto Bruno, nipote di Vito Bruno Lanza.

descriptions: The two batteries are historically opposed but cooperate in racketeering through a common fund.

I due gruppi criminali storicamente in contrapposizione infatti sul fronte del racket chiudevano affari in sinergia e si spartivano i ricavi grazie ad una “cassa comune”.
